scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Taylor logs into the state UI portal; Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Taylor Nguyen opens the state UI portal to submit their weekly certification. Because UI claims are fraud-sensitive — identity impersonation and duplicate claims across states are known fraud patterns — Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login itself. Biometric proof of identity established at the session level, before any certification action begins. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Taylor hits the state UI portal login.
beats.lede: Username and password. But instead of a standard session, Maestro intercepts and invokes CLEAR — a step-up for UI benefit access.

beats.head: CLEAR is selected — SSA + multi-state check runs.
beats.lede: One tap. CLEAR verifies Taylor's identity, cross-references with SSA records, and checks for overlapping claims in other state UI systems simultaneously.

beats.head: Mobile handoff to Taylor's phone.
beats.lede: CLEAR session opens on the device. ID scan, selfie, biometric match — the whole verification runs in under two minutes, no office visit required.

beats.head: ID and SSA match captured; no duplicate-claim flags.
beats.lede: CLEAR verifies Taylor's government-issued ID, confirms the SSA match, and returns clean on the multi-state overlap check. Result posts back in seconds.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens; biometric proof bound to the session.
beats.lede: Zero duplicate claims detected. Taylor's session is now authenticated and fraud-gated. The portal unlocks to the certification flow. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Taylor submits the weekly cert; Maestro serves the form — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside the authenticated session, Taylor opens "File Weekly Certification." Maestro renders the weekly-cert webform inline — pre-filled with prior-week data from the claim record and the F1 IDV identity binding. No second IDV. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Taylor opens the certification flow.
beats.lede: The weekly-cert form appears pre-populated with last week's job-search activity, availability status, and hours available. Taylor reviews and confirms or edits as needed.

beats.head: Maestro pre-fills from prior certification and claim record.
beats.lede: Availability hours, job-search activities, and earnings since last cert are already in the form — drawn from the claim file and prior-week data. Taylor only enters what changed.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens for anomalies or policy violations.
beats.lede: Availability hours vs. reported earnings; comparison to the claim's initial benefit estimate; any work-search flagging. The AI runs silently in the background.

beats.head: Taylor reviews the totals and submits.
beats.lede: Weekly hours confirmed, earnings reported, job-search attestation given. Submit fires — bound to the existing biometric proof from F1. No additional verification needed.

beats.head: Submit fires — bound to the established biometric proof.
beats.lede: Witness event #2 logged. The adjudicator's queue is updated; payment release or hold determined automatically based on the cert content and Iris flagging.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the CLEAR auth event and the certification submission as one continuous identity thread.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been linking the auth event to the certification submission in the orchestration layer the whole time. Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible. The DOL's fraud team sees the unified record: the CLEAR biometric authentication at portal entry, and the weekly certification — linked as a continuous identity thread. One audit trail. One defensible record. The chain-of-custody gap closes.

beats.head: Agreement Desk opens to Taylor's certification activity.
beats.lede: The CLEAR auth event (time, ID match, SSA cross-ref, multi-state check) and the weekly-cert submission sit in a single view.

beats.head: The link is explicit: auth proof → certification.
beats.lede: "Verified by CLEAR · Taylor Nguyen · SSA match · multi-state clean · 09:15 EST" → "Weekly cert filed · 8 hours · $480 earnings · 09:22 EST."

beats.head: Iris confirms pattern match or flags anomaly.
beats.lede: The cert content is consistent with prior weeks and the claim's initial parameters. No fraud signal. The certification queues for auto-approval.

beats.head: Payment determination issued automatically.
beats.lede: The claim's adjudication engine processes the certification. Payment release or hold triggered based on the cert data and eligibility status.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If the certification is ever audited or disputed, the DOL has both halves of the chain: identity verification at the moment of claim action, and the action itself, linked by continuous audit trail.

intake.subtitle: × Taylor Nguyen — Initial UI Claim Self-File

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE WEBFORM
scenes.head: Taylor files the initial UI claim via state portal — self-service from home.
scenes.lede: Taylor Nguyen opens the state UI portal on a phone the morning after the layoff. A mobile-first webform walks through claim filing — last employer, separation date, separation reason, last wages. Questions pre-filled from the state's employer database where possible. Taylor is the applicant here, filing self-service.

beats.head: Taylor opens "File a New Claim."
beats.lede: The webform auto-detects their location. Employer name search returns the list of their last three employers. Taylor selects the recent layoff.

beats.head: Employer data pre-fills base information.
beats.lede: Employer name, address, and industry auto-populate. Taylor enters the last-day-worked and separation reason (reduction in force).

beats.head: Last-wage questions guide the income recapture.
beats.lede: Prior 52 weeks of W-2 data is already queried from the IRS record in parallel. The form shows last-quarter wages and asks for any unreported side income. Taylor reports none.

beats.head: Availability and work-search selections branch the package.
beats.lede: Can work full-time? Yes. Limitations? None. Active job search? Yes. The form's questions determine what documents Maestro will assemble.

beats.head: Taylor reviews the configured claim and submits.
beats.lede: The form summarizes: claim type, base-period wages pre-calculated from W-2 data, weekly benefit estimate, and the documents Maestro will send for signature.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out the claim determination packet from Taylor's responses.
scenes.lede: Submit fires. Maestro pulls the right claim documents based on Taylor's answers (full-time availability, no limitations, active work search), pre-fills from the W-2 records and the claim form, builds the recipient list, and routes the packet to Taylor for signature.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the right determination packet template.
beats.lede: Taylor's answers (full availability, no limitations, work-search confirmation) map to the standard claim packet: eligibility determination, direct-deposit authorization, and work-search agreement.

beats.head: W-2 data pre-fills the base-period calculation.
beats.lede: Last four quarters of reported wages from the IRS record populate the wage history fields. No manual request to the employer needed.

beats.head: Weekly benefit amount calculated and inserted.
beats.lede: From base-period wages, the benefit formula is applied. The determination letter shows the weekly amount and maximum duration.

beats.head: Recipient routing built: Taylor first, adjudicator second.
beats.lede: The determination packet routes to Taylor for signature and any required attestation, then auto-routes to the assigned adjudicator for final review.

beats.head: Packet ready for delivery.
beats.lede: Envelope assembled, signature flow sequenced, audit trail pre-staged. Maestro queues the delivery.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Taylor signs the determination, direct-deposit auth, and work-search agreement in one ceremony.
scenes.lede: The packet arrives in seconds — state-branded, three forms in order. Taylor verifies identity during signing (standard OTP for self-service intake), and the documents are executed. The adjudicator's queue is updated immediately.

beats.head: State-branded email lands in Taylor's inbox.
beats.lede: One link, one call-to-action. No separate account or portal login required for self-service claimants.

beats.head: Signing ceremony opens with the three documents in order.
beats.lede: Eligibility determination, direct-deposit authorization, and work-search agreement. Yellow START tab guides Taylor through.

beats.head: Identity verified via OTP at signing time.
beats.lede: For self-service claims, a one-time code confirms Taylor's phone number. No second CLEAR required; OTP is sufficient for intake verification.

beats.head: One signature applied across all three documents.
beats.lede: Signature adopted; same mark on determination, DD auth, and work-search agreement.

beats.head: Adjudicator queue updated; claim moved to determination review.
beats.lede: The claim file is marked "ready for final review." The assigned adjudicator receives the complete packet — W-2 data, webform responses, executed documents — in seconds.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING CLAIM RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Taylor and the adjudicator share claim status in one Workspace.
scenes.lede: Initial claim determined, weekly certification schedule set. Taylor receives a Workspace invite — the single surface for the entire claim relationship. Weekly certs, payment status, benefit modifications, appeals — all in one place.

beats.head: Taylor receives the claim Workspace invite.
beats.lede: State-branded email with a one-tap link. No app to download, no separate login. Taylor and the adjudicator are now in the same Workspace.

beats.head: Claim status and payment history visible from day one.
beats.lede: Overview shows the eligibility determination, weekly benefit amount, maximum duration, and first payment date. Tasks shows the next certification due date.

beats.head: Iris in the rail tracks the claim's activity.
beats.lede: Every adjudication event, every certification filed, every payment released — visible in the activity log.

beats.head: Weekly cert task opens automatically on schedule.
beats.lede: The next certification form appears as a task when the filing window opens. Pre-filled with job-search activity from the prior week, ready for Taylor to review and submit.

beats.head: Claim active — the relationship runs from here.
beats.lede: Initial claim executed, first payment queued, claimant and adjudicator in the same Workspace. The DOL's fraud team has the complete verification and certification audit trail.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Marissa logs into Springfield's permit portal; Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Marissa Rivera opens Springfield Community Development's permit portal to request a modification on her property at 1420 Oak Street. Property-modification requests can impact zoning compliance and fee exposure — Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login to establish identity proof before any permit action begins. Ownership verification tied to the session. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Marissa hits the permit portal login.
beats.lede: Username and password. Maestro intercepts and invokes CLEAR — a step-up for permit modifications.

beats.head: CLEAR is selected — property ownership verified.
beats.lede: One tap. CLEAR verifies Marissa's identity and cross-references her against the County Assessor's record for 1420 Oak Street to confirm ownership.

beats.head: Mobile handoff to Marissa's phone.
beats.lede: CLEAR session opens on her device. ID scan, selfie, biometric match, ownership confirmation — all verified without a trip to City Hall.

beats.head: ID and ownership match captured.
beats.lede: CLEAR confirms Marissa is the record owner of the property. Title verification results post back in seconds.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens; ownership proof bound to the session.
beats.lede: The portal unlocks to the property-specific actions. Marissa can now file modifications with verified ownership tied to every action. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Marissa requests a permit modification; Maestro serves the form — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside the authenticated session, Marissa opens "Modify Permit." Maestro renders the permit-modification webform inline — pre-filled with the existing permit data (address, current scope, original fee) and the F1 ownership-verified identity binding. No second verification. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Marissa opens "Modify Existing Permit."
beats.lede: Her active permits for 1420 Oak Street appear. She selects the framing-and-roofing permit to request an expanded scope.

beats.head: Maestro pre-fills the modification form.
beats.lede: Original permit details (scope, fee, zoning district) are already in the form. Marissa enters the new scope addition — exterior deck extension — and the revised valuation.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens for zoning and fee implications.
beats.lede: The scope change is checked against setback requirements and fee-schedule rules. The system calculates the additional permit fee and flags any potential zoning issues for engineering review.

beats.head: Marissa reviews the modification summary and submits.
beats.lede: Original scope, new addition, revised valuation, and recalculated fee are all visible. Marissa confirms the changes and submits for plan-check re-engagement.

beats.head: Submit fires — bound to the established ownership proof.
beats.lede: Witness event #2 logged. The modification request queues for engineering review. The plan-check clock resets.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the ownership-verification event and the modification submission as one continuous property record.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been linking the ownership verification to the modification submission in the orchestration layer the whole time. Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible. Springfield's permitting team sees the unified record: the CLEAR ownership verification at portal entry, and the permit-modification request — linked as a continuous property-control thread. One audit trail. One defensible record.

beats.head: Agreement Desk opens to Marissa's modification request.
beats.lede: The CLEAR ownership-verification event (time, ID match, County Assessor confirmation) and the modification submission sit in a single view.

beats.head: The link is explicit: ownership proof → modification.
beats.lede: "Verified by CLEAR · Marissa Rivera · CA DL ***3847 · County Assessor confirmed · 14:12 PST" → "Permit modification filed · scope addition · $2,400 fee · 14:19 PST."

beats.head: Plan Check Engineer reviews the ownership + scope modification together.
beats.lede: The engineer has both the verified ownership and the modified scope in one view. Zoning review and fee confirmation happen in context.

beats.head: Modified permit moves to plan-check queue.
beats.lede: The modification is accepted with the ownership proof already confirmed. Re-engineering begins immediately.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If the modification is ever challenged or audited, Springfield has both halves of the chain: ownership verification tied to identity, and the modification action, linked by continuous audit trail.

intake.subtitle: × Marissa Rivera — Residential Permit Application Self-File

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE WEBFORM
scenes.head: Marissa files the permit application via Springfield's portal — self-service from home.
scenes.lede: Marissa Rivera opens Springfield Community Development's permit portal to file a new residential building permit for 1420 Oak Street. A mobile-first webform walks through the application — property address, scope of work, contractor info, valuation, and any site plans. Marissa is the applicant, filing self-service.

beats.head: Marissa opens "Apply for a New Permit."
beats.lede: Property address search returns 1420 Oak Street. The parcel profile (zoning, lot size, use history) auto-populates from the assessor database.

beats.head: Scope of work questions branch the application.
beats.lede: Marissa selects "residential remodel — kitchen and bathroom." Follow-up questions ask for construction value, planned contractor, and estimated duration.

beats.head: Contractor license search and fee calculation run in parallel.
beats.lede: As Marissa enters the contractor name, the form auto-verifies the license against the state licensing board. The permit fee is calculated based on valuation and zoning district.

beats.head: Site plan upload and pre-screening.
beats.lede: Marissa uploads PDFs of the site plan and building drawings. The system runs a quick structural scan — dimensions, lot coverage, setback estimates — to pre-flag any obvious zoning conflicts.

beats.head: Marissa reviews the configured package and submits.
beats.lede: Application summary shows: property, scope, contractor verified, fee calculated, site plan uploaded, documents ready for signature.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out the permit application packet from Marissa's responses.
scenes.lede: Submit fires. Maestro pulls the right permit documents (application, site-plan acknowledgment, fee authorization, contractor attestation) based on Marissa's scope, pre-fills from the assessor and contractor records, routes the packet to Marissa for signature, and queues it for engineering plan check.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the application form and supporting docs.
beats.lede: Residential remodel template with kitchen-and-bathroom scope, site-plan acknowledgment, fee authorization for the calculated amount, and contractor attestation.

beats.head: Assessor data pre-fills property fields.
beats.lede: Address, parcel number, zoning district, lot size — all auto-populated from Springfield's property database. No re-keying.

beats.head: Contractor license and insurance confirmation auto-attach.
beats.lede: The verified contractor's license number and C/O insurance certificate auto-populate the contractor-attestation document.

beats.head: Recipient routing built: Marissa first, plan-check team second.
beats.lede: The application packet routes to Marissa for signature and fee authorization, then auto-routes to Springfield's engineering department for plan check.

beats.head: Packet ready for delivery.
beats.lede: Envelope assembled, signature flow sequenced, pre-check results staged. Maestro queues the delivery.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Marissa signs the application, site-plan acknowledgment, and fee authorization in one ceremony.
scenes.lede: The packet arrives in seconds — Springfield-branded, four forms in order. Marissa verifies ownership during signing, and the documents are executed. Plan check is triggered immediately, and Marissa receives the permit number the same day.

beats.head: Springfield-branded email lands in Marissa's inbox.
beats.lede: One link, one call-to-action. Pre-populated with her address and the application summary.

beats.head: Signing ceremony opens with the four documents in order.
beats.lede: Application, site-plan acknowledgment, fee authorization, and contractor attestation. Yellow START tab guides Marissa through.

beats.head: Ownership verified via CLEAR at signing time.
beats.lede: For permit intake, Marissa verifies her identity and ownership of 1420 Oak Street inline. The proof is bound to the executed application.

beats.head: One signature applied across all four documents.
beats.lede: Signature adopted across application, acknowledgment, fee authorization, and attestation.

beats.head: Plan-check queue updated; permit assigned to engineering.
beats.lede: The complete application file — property record, scope, contractor, site plan, executed documents — arrives in the engineering team's queue. Plan check begins immediately.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING PERMIT RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Marissa and Springfield's engineering team share permit status in one Workspace.
scenes.lede: Permit application filed and assigned to plan check. Marissa receives a Workspace invite — the single surface for the entire permit process. Inspections, corrections, CO issuance — all visible to both parties, updated in real time.

beats.head: Marissa receives the permit Workspace invite.
beats.lede: Springfield-branded email with a one-tap link. Marissa can see the inspection schedule, plan-check status, and any correction requests as the permit progresses.

beats.head: Permit status and inspection schedule visible from day one.
beats.lede: Overview shows the application summary, assigned inspector, and estimated review timeline. Tasks shows upcoming inspections and what each will cover.

beats.head: Iris tracks the permit in the activity log.
beats.lede: Every plan-check milestone, every inspection visit, every correction request — visible to Marissa and the engineering team in one place.

beats.head: Inspection task opens when the review is scheduled.
beats.lede: The inspection checklist, scheduled date, and required corrections appear as tasks. Marissa and her contractor see the same requirements.

beats.head: Permit active — the relationship runs from here.
beats.lede: Application executed, plan check in progress, owner and city in the same Workspace. Springfield's records team has the complete permit file with audit trail intact.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Maria Cortez — Follow-Up on Open Requests

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Maria logs into Greenhaven's 311 citizen portal; Maestro orchestrates light-touch CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Maria Cortez opens Greenhaven Public Works' 311 citizen portal to check on a previous pothole report and possibly add a follow-up. Multiple reports from the same citizen are legitimate, but duplicate fraud (fake updates to game response-time metrics) is a known 311 risk. Maestro orchestrates a light-touch CLEAR at login — biometric proof of identity tied to the citizen's service-request account. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Maria hits the 311 citizen portal login.
beats.lede: Phone number and one-time code. Maestro intercepts and invokes a light-touch CLEAR — sufficient for citizen-side identity, not the full DEA-grade verification.

beats.head: CLEAR is selected — citizen identity confirmed.
beats.lede: One tap. CLEAR verifies Maria's ID and binds the session to her phone number and email. No second device or manual approval needed.

beats.head: Mobile handoff to Maria's phone.
beats.lede: CLEAR session opens on her device. ID scan and selfie — quick verification. No office visit, no chatbot queue required.

beats.head: ID verified; session bound to citizen record.
beats.lede: CLEAR confirms Maria's identity. The 311 system links the session to her prior service requests and contact info.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens; identity proof bound to the account.
beats.lede: Maria can now view her prior reports and file follow-ups with verified identity tied to every request. Duplicate-fraud signals are easier to detect. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Maria adds a follow-up; Maestro serves the update form — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside the authenticated session, Maria opens her prior pothole report (SR-2026-71248 from last week) and clicks "Add Update." Maestro renders the request-update webform inline — pre-filled with the prior report's location, category, and photos — plus the F1 identity binding. No second verification. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Maria views her prior pothole report.
beats.lede: The old report (date filed, location, status) appears with a "related report or follow-up" option.

beats.head: Maestro pre-fills the update form.
beats.lede: Location (same GPS pin), issue type (pothole, pre-filled), photo upload field — ready for Maria to add new evidence if the repair isn't visible.

beats.head: Maria uploads a new photo or text update.
beats.lede: She adds a note: "Pothole still visible on the south side of the street. The cold-mix repair is cracking." Optional photo attachment.

beats.head: Iris checks for report fraud patterns.
beats.lede: Same citizen, same location, same category, elapsed time since last report — all consistent with a legitimate follow-up, not duplicate fraud.

beats.head: Submit fires — bound to the established citizen identity.
beats.lede: Witness event #2 logged. The update queues to the Operations Center and the assigned Cedar Ave crew for re-evaluation.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · GREENHAVEN'S OPERATIONS DESK LINKS THE EVENTS
scenes.head: Operations Desk surfaces the citizen's identity verification and the follow-up request as one continuous service thread.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been linking the citizen's identity to each service request in the orchestration layer the whole time. The Operations Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible. Greenhaven's Ops team sees the unified record: the CLEAR citizen identity at portal entry, and the follow-up report — linked as a continuous service-request thread. One citizen, one location, one audit trail. The chain-of-custody gap closes.

beats.head: Operations Desk opens to Maria's service history.
beats.lede: The CLEAR citizen identity verification (time, ID match, phone confirmation) and the follow-up update sit in a single view, linked to the prior SR-2026-71248.

beats.head: The link is explicit: citizen verified → follow-up filed.
beats.lede: "Verified by CLEAR · Maria Cortez · CA ID ***7234 · phone confirmed · 16:45 PST" → "Follow-up filed on SR-2026-71248 · pothole still visible · new photo added · 16:52 PST."

beats.head: Iris flags the report quality and priority.
beats.lede: Same location, reasonable elapsed time, new photographic evidence — legitimate follow-up, high priority for the crew re-visit.

beats.head: Cedar Ave crew receives the updated work order.
beats.lede: SR-2026-71248-UPDATE pushes to the crew's queue with the new photo and Maria's note. Re-repair is prioritized over new reports on the same day.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If the repair quality is ever audited or if Maria escalates, Greenhaven has both halves of the chain: citizen identity verified, and the follow-up request, linked by continuous audit trail.

intake.subtitle: × Maria Cortez — Pothole Report via QR Code

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE WEBFORM VIA QR
scenes.head: Maria scans a 311 QR code on-site and files the pothole report — no account needed.
scenes.lede: Maria Cortez walks past a pothole on Cedar Avenue and scans a 311 QR code on the nearest utility box. A mobile webform opens pre-tagged with the GPS location from the QR scan. Maria selects "pothole," adds a photo of the damage, and submits. Maria is the citizen reporter; no account creation required.

beats.head: Maria scans the on-site 311 QR code.
beats.lede: A link opens in her phone's browser. No app to download, no Greenhaven account required. The form is already geo-tagged with the QR code's location (1124 Cedar Ave).

beats.head: Geo-tagged form appears with location pre-filled.
beats.lede: "Report a problem at this location" — the street and cross-street are already in the form from the QR scan. Maria taps to confirm or adjust the pin.

beats.head: Issue category dropdown: pothole selected.
beats.lede: Maria selects from the list (pothole, graffiti, streetlight, tree, etc.). Selecting "pothole" shows priority hints — "18+ inches = high priority."

beats.head: Photo upload and size estimate.
beats.lede: Maria takes a photo with her phone. The form includes optional text: "Size of a dinner plate — south side of street." Maestro runs image-size estimation on the photo.

beats.head: Maria submits as a guest — no account sign-up.
beats.lede: One-tap submit. The report goes to the Ops Center with the GPS pin, photo, category, and risk-scoring data.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out the dispatch packet from Maria's report.
scenes.lede: Submit fires. Maestro auto-classifies the report (issue type, severity, location), assigns the on-rotation crew, pulls the work-order template, pre-fills location and materials estimate, and queues the SMS to the assigned crew with the GPS pin and photo.

beats.head: Maestro auto-classifies: Streets · Pavement · Pothole.
beats.lede: Issue type from Maria's selection, severity assessment from the photo and her description. Priority routing determined.

beats.head: On-rotation crew assignment (Cedar Ave team) confirmed.
beats.lede: Maestro pulls the crew roster and their current assignment load. The Cedar Ave team is assigned the dispatch.

beats.head: Work order assembled with materials manifest.
beats.lede: From the severity estimate, Maestro selects the cold-mix cold-patch repair kit and estimated materials quantity. No manual materials request needed.

beats.head: GPS pin, photo, and ETA sent to crew via SMS.
beats.lede: The Cedar Ave crew receives SMS with the location, Maria's photo, and estimated travel time. Work order details available in the Workspace.

beats.head: Maria receives confirmation with tracking number SR-2026-71248.
beats.lede: SMS reply lands within 30 seconds with the ticket number and expected resolution window (same day).

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · COMPLETION AND CITIZEN NOTIFICATION
scenes.head: Cedar Ave crew completes the repair; Maria gets closed-loop confirmation with the after-photo.
scenes.lede: The crew arrives on-site, completes the pothole repair, and the crew lead signs the work-completion attestation from the truck. Maria receives an SMS with the after-photo and an instant "rate this resolution" link — no callback needed, no app, no account required.

beats.head: Cedar Ave crew arrives and confirms the location.
beats.lede: Crew lead verifies the GPS pin matches Maria's report. Before and after photos will be captured.

beats.head: Pothole repair completed with standard cold-mix.
beats.lede: Materials (cold-patch mix, labor minutes) recorded. Before and after photos uploaded directly into the work order — no separate camera, no email needed.

beats.head: Work-completion attestation signed from the truck.
beats.lede: One document — completion attestation with materials, time on site, before/after photos — signed by the crew lead on the city-issued device. OTP authentication ties the signature to the verified worker.

beats.head: Maria receives closed-loop SMS with after-photo and rating link.
beats.lede: SMS from the same tracking number thread: after-photo, completion message, one-tap "rate this resolution" link. No follow-up call required.

beats.head: Service request auto-closed with full audit trail.
beats.lede: SR-2026-71248 closes with the complete evidence trail — geo-tagged report, work order, materials, time on site, completion photos, citizen rating. The Cedar Ave repair crew's daily report writes itself.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Yolanda Rivera — Change of Household Composition

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Yolanda logs into Eastside's benefits portal; Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Yolanda Rivera opens Eastside Health & Human Services' benefits portal to report a mid-year change of household composition. SNAP and related benefit changes involve identity-sensitive data and recipient impersonation is a known fraud surface. Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login to establish biometric identity proof before any household-change action begins. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Yolanda hits the benefits portal login.
beats.lede: Username and password. Maestro intercepts and invokes CLEAR — a step-up for household changes.

beats.head: CLEAR is selected — SSA + household verification runs.
beats.lede: One tap. CLEAR verifies Yolanda's identity and cross-references against SSA records to confirm she's the account holder. Household members on the account are queried from the prior application.

beats.head: Mobile handoff to Yolanda's phone.
beats.lede: CLEAR session opens on her device. ID scan, selfie, biometric match, SSA confirmation — all verified without a trip to the eligibility office.

beats.head: ID and SSA match captured; account holder confirmed.
beats.lede: CLEAR verifies Yolanda as the account holder and pulls the household composition from the prior SNAP application. Result posts back in seconds.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens; biometric identity proof bound to the account.
beats.lede: The portal unlocks to household-change actions. Yolanda can now report changes with verified identity tied to every action. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Yolanda reports a household change; Maestro serves the form — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside the authenticated session, Yolanda opens "Update Household Information." Maestro renders the change-of-circumstance webform inline — pre-filled with the current household from the prior application and the F1 IDV identity binding. No second verification. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Yolanda opens "Update Household."
beats.lede: The form appears with the current household members already listed from her prior application. She can add/remove members or note changes in circumstances.

beats.head: Maestro pre-fills from the prior application.
beats.lede: Household members, ages, relationship, and employment status from the last certified application are in the form. Yolanda only needs to enter what changed.

beats.head: Yolanda reports the change — a new dependent.
beats.lede: She adds a new household member and indicates the effective date. The form asks clarifying questions (birth date, relationship, income status). Iris runs eligibility simulation in the background.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens for benefit-impact implications.
beats.lede: The addition of a household member changes SNAP benefit amounts and may open eligibility for other programs (WIC, LIHEAP). The AI flags the cross-program implications and queues them for Devon's review.

beats.head: Submit fires — bound to the established identity.
beats.lede: Witness event #2 logged. The change-of-circumstance report queues for eligibility re-determination. Yolanda's case is flagged for fast-track processing.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · EASTSIDE'S CASE WORKSPACE LINKS THE EVENTS
scenes.head: Eastside's case Workspace surfaces the identity verification and the household change as one continuous record.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been linking the identity verification to the household-change submission in the orchestration layer the whole time. Eastside's case management surface is the proxy that makes that invisible work visible. Yolanda's case Workspace shows the unified record: the CLEAR identity verification at portal entry, and the household-change submission — linked as a continuous case thread. One audit trail. One defensible record. The chain-of-custody gap closes.

beats.head: Yolanda's case Workspace shows the identity verification + change report.
beats.lede: The CLEAR identity event (time, ID match, SSA confirmation) and the household-change submission sit in a single view, linked to the prior application.

beats.head: The link is explicit: identity verified → household change.
beats.lede: "Verified by CLEAR · Yolanda Rivera · SSA match · account holder confirmed · 10:15 PST" → "Household change filed · new dependent added · 10:22 PST."

beats.head: Devon's eligibility team reviews the change in context.
beats.lede: Devon has both the verified identity and the household change in one view. Re-determination eligibility and any cross-program openings are flagged automatically.

beats.head: Eligibility determination re-runs; new benefit amount calculated.
beats.lede: SNAP benefit increased based on the new household size. WIC eligibility opened for the new child. LIHEAP utility-assistance eligibility checked.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If the benefit change is ever audited or disputed, Eastside has both halves of the chain: identity verification tied to the account holder, and the household change, linked by continuous audit trail.

intake.subtitle: × Yolanda Rivera — SNAP Application Self-File from Mobile

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE MOBILE WEBFORM
scenes.head: Yolanda files the SNAP application via mobile portal — self-service from home.
scenes.lede: Yolanda Rivera opens Eastside Health & Human Services' mobile-first portal to apply for SNAP after a recent income loss. The application webform is optimized for a phone — household composition, income sources, rent/utilities, and any prior program touchpoints with the agency. Yolanda is the applicant, filing self-service.

beats.head: Yolanda opens "Apply for SNAP."
beats.lede: The mobile form asks for basic info first — household size, primary language, address. Fields are large, keyboard-friendly, save-and-resume enabled.

beats.head: Household members added one by one with relationship and age.
beats.lede: Yolanda lists herself, her spouse (if applicable), and the children. Age and relationship determine which programs each member qualifies for.

beats.head: Income sources captured — employment, child support, SSI.
beats.lede: Last 30 days of income, prior W-2s, any expected income changes. The form branches based on income level — if below threshold, WIC and LIHEAP questions appear.

beats.head: Household expenses (rent, utilities, childcare) pre-assessed.
beats.lede: Monthly rent/mortgage, utilities, childcare costs. These affect SNAP deductions and determine LIHEAP eligibility.

beats.head: Yolanda reviews and submits.
beats.lede: Form summary shows household size, estimated monthly SNAP benefit, cross-program eligibility flags, and next steps (identity verification and signing).

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out the SNAP application packet from Yolanda's responses.
scenes.lede: Submit fires. Maestro pulls the right eligibility documents based on Yolanda's household composition and income — SNAP application, household composition attestation, income disclosure — pre-fills from her answers and any prior agency records, routes the packet to Yolanda for signature, and queues it for Devon's eligibility review.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the SNAP application + supporting docs.
beats.lede: Application form, household composition attestation, and income-disclosure form. Templates select based on family size and income tier.

beats.head: Prior agency data pre-fills where available.
beats.lede: If Yolanda has prior SNAP or Medicaid records with Eastside, household and address data auto-populate. No re-entry of what's already known.

beats.head: Income fields pre-filled from her webform submission.
beats.lede: Employment, child support, SSI — all copied from the intake webform into the signed application. Yolanda only needs to verify, not re-type.

beats.head: Cross-program eligibility questions auto-attach.
beats.lede: Based on household size and income, WIC and LIHEAP eligibility screeners are included in the packet for Yolanda to review pre-signing.

beats.head: Recipient routing built: Yolanda first, Devon second.
beats.lede: The application packet routes to Yolanda for signature and verification, then auto-routes to Devon's eligibility queue for determination.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Yolanda signs the application, household attestation, and income disclosure in one ceremony.
scenes.lede: The packet arrives in seconds — Eastside-branded, three forms in order optimized for mobile. Yolanda verifies identity during signing (CLEAR for intake verification), and the documents are executed. Devon's eligibility queue is updated immediately.

beats.head: Eastside-branded email/SMS lands in Yolanda's inbox.
beats.lede: One link, mobile-optimized. Pre-populated with her address and the application summary. Text option available for SMS-only access.

beats.head: Signing ceremony opens with the three documents in order.
beats.lede: SNAP application, household composition attestation, income disclosure. Yellow START tab guides Yolanda through on her phone.

beats.head: Identity verified via CLEAR at signing time.
beats.lede: For intake verification, Yolanda verifies her identity and SSA cross-reference inline. The proof is bound to the executed application.

beats.head: One signature applied across all three documents.
beats.lede: Signature adopted across application, attestation, and disclosure. The signature flow is streamlined for mobile — large signature area, single tap to finalize.

beats.head: Eligibility queue updated; determination review begins.
beats.lede: The complete application file — household, income, prior-program history (if any), executed documents — arrives in Devon's queue. Determination is issued within hours for fast-track cases.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING FAMILY RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Yolanda and Eastside's eligibility team share benefits status in one Workspace.
scenes.lede: SNAP determined and EBT card is on the way. Yolanda receives a Workspace invite — the single surface for the entire family's benefit relationship. Recertification, mid-year changes, program additions, EBT issues — all visible to both parties.

beats.head: Yolanda receives the family benefits Workspace invite.
beats.lede: Eastside-branded email and SMS option. One-tap link; no app to download, no separate account login.

beats.head: Benefits status and determination letter visible from day one.
beats.lede: Overview shows SNAP approval, monthly benefit amount, EBT card delivery date, and any cross-program tasks (WIC enrollment, LIHEAP application). Yolanda sees the same state as Devon.

beats.head: Iris tracks the case in the activity log.
beats.lede: Every determination, benefit change, recertification, and mid-year report — visible in one place. Yolanda and Devon both see full history.

beats.head: WIC enrollment task opens if Yolanda qualifies.
beats.lede: The WIC application (pre-filled from the SNAP record) appears as a task. Yolanda only needs to confirm the youngest child's pediatrician and submit — no re-entry of household data.

beats.head: Family enrolled — relationship established.
beats.lede: SNAP active, EBT card on the way, WIC opened (if applicable), family and agency in the same Workspace. Eastside's compliance team has the complete audit trail and can answer questions without case-file lookups.
